scenarios.task: A stakeholder requested 'a leadership communication skills course' for their team. I'm opening the authoring tool now and building an outline directly from that request, since it's a clear enough topic to start designing content around.
criteria: Flags building directly from the stated topic without needs-assessment fieldwork as the skipping-the-needs-assessment failure mode
States that 'we need training on X' is a solution statement, not a gap statement, and the actual gap needs to be established through fieldwork (SME interviews, task analysis, person-level gap data)
Recommends running a needs assessment across organizational, task, and person levels before building content
Notes the risk of building a course that answers the wrong gap, discoverable only after low results roll in

scenarios.task: The terminal objective for my new course is written as 'employees will understand the company's data privacy policy.' I'm using this as the objective driving the course design and assessment.
criteria: Flags the topic-style objective as ungradable, naming the writing-objectives-as-topics failure mode
States that a well-formed objective needs a condition, observable behavior, and criterion (Mager's format), and 'understand X' can't be assessed
Recommends rewriting the objective as a specific observable behavior with a criterion (e.g. given a specific data-handling scenario, correctly apply a specific rule within a stated standard)
States that an ungradable objective also can't tell you whether the course worked

scenarios.task: The objective for this course requires associates to de-escalate a live, emotionally charged customer interaction using specific verbal techniques and real-time judgment. Since eLearning is cheaper and faster to build than instructor-led training, I'm building this as a click-through eLearning module.
criteria: Flags defaulting to eLearning for an interpersonal/psychomotor skill based on cost/speed alone as the choosing-eLearning-by-default failure mode
States that a live, emotionally charged interpersonal skill needs role-play/ILT fidelity that a click-through module can't simulate
Recommends selecting delivery format based on the objective's required practice fidelity, not primarily on cost or build speed
Notes the risk: the module transmits information about the skill but the learner still can't perform it live

scenarios.task: Our new compliance eLearning module shows a 94% completion rate in the LMS dashboard. I'm reporting this to leadership as strong evidence that the training was effective and employees learned the material.
criteria: Flags treating completion rate as evidence of learning/behavior change as the failure mode
States that LMS completion percentage is an enrollment/click-through metric, not a Kirkpatrick Level 2 or 3 measure of learning or behavior
Recommends reporting Level 2/3/4 data (knowledge assessment scores, behavior observation, business metric) alongside completion, not completion alone
States that this is the most common way LMS reporting overstates program effectiveness
